reasoning: The historical average of Strategic developments in Sweden over the past 360 days is 2 per month. To reach a threshold that is >10x the baseline value of 3 (calculated as 1 + 2), there would need to be 31 or more Strategic developments in a single 30-day period. This would constitute an extraordinary 15-fold increase in the frequency of such events in a stable country like Sweden, meaning more than one strategic development (e.g., massive arrests, border build-ups, major agreements) per day. Recent news shows gang violence and related arrests, but ACLED typically groups related single-day raids into combined events, and such spikes have not driven up the historical average significantly (which remains at a very low 2 per month). The very short time frames for the April resolution dates make this essentially impossible, and probabilities remain low throughout 2025 as structural conditions inside Sweden are unlikely to change violently enough to cross this very high threshold.
